metadata.title: Why 'Never Generate a Grade' Fails Until You Write a Hook

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I watched Maria's NEVER rule lose the vote to a live prompt, and I want the hook that would have won it. For a fresh project: (1) explain how the PreToolUse hook reads the proposed tool input on stdin before the Write happens; (2) help me write the grade-pattern match that exits non-zero with a BLOCKED message; (3) show me exactly where the script lives in .claude/hooks/ and how it gets registered in settings.json so it fires every session.

props.command: I watched Maria's NEVER rule lose the vote to a live prompt, and I want the hook that would have won it. For a fresh project: (1) explain how the PreToolUse hook reads the proposed tool input on stdin before the Write happens; (2) help me write the grade-pattern match that exits non-zero with a BLOCKED message; (3) show me exactly where the script lives in .claude/hooks/ and how it gets registered in settings.json so it fires every session.

beats.narration_text: Maria types it in all caps. Under a heading that says NEVER. And two sessions later, Claude writes the grade anyway.

card.copy: Why 'Never Generate a Grade' fails until you write a Hook

beats.narration_text: She opens CLAUDE.md and writes it once, clearly. Never generate a final grade. Numeric and letter grades are the teacher's responsibility. She saves it and moves on.

production_viz.label: CLAUDE.md with the NEVER rule
production_viz.mechanic: a document card fades in with a NEVER heading and the rule text beneath; a serif label 'advisory' appears below the card

beats.narration_text: Session three. She asks Claude to summarize a student's performance. Claude reads CLAUDE.md. Weights the instruction. And the immediate prompt pulls harder.

production_viz.label: probability dial — the instruction gets a weight, not a lock
production_viz.mechanic: a dial labeled 'follow CLAUDE.md rule' starts high but drifts toward lower as a second force labeled 'immediate prompt' pushes from the right; the needle settles in the high-but-not-absolute zone

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] probability dial showing the rule is weighted, not locked

beats.narration_text: The file saves. student-07.md opens. The last line reads: Overall performance: B-plus. The NEVER rule did not fail. It lost the vote.

production_viz.label: the grade appears in the file
production_viz.mechanic: a file card labeled 'student-07.md' with a final line appearing in crimson: 'Overall performance: B+'; a small LabelChip labeled 'NEVER rule' sits dimmed above it

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the grade written despite the rule

beats.narration_text: Here is the question. A CLAUDE.md instruction should prevent grade generation. Here is the session where it did not. Why?

card.copy: A CLAUDE.md rule said NEVER. The grade appeared anyway. Why?

beats.narration_text: CLAUDE.md is advisory. Claude reads it at session start, weighs it against everything else in context, and follows it with high probability — but not absolute certainty. The more content in the session, the lower weight the standing instruction carries.

production_viz.label: the advisory path — high probability, not guarantee
production_viz.mechanic: a vertical lane labeled 'CLAUDE.md advisory path' with a probability meter; as session tokens accumulate (small blocks stacking), the meter dips from 95 to 87 to 80; a crimson dot labeled 'violation' appears at the bottom

beats.narration_text: A Hook is different. It is a script that runs before Claude uses a tool — before the Write happens. Claude proposes the action. The hook inspects it. If the content matches a grade pattern, the hook exits non-zero. The Write does not happen. Claude's decision is irrelevant.

production_viz.label: the hook gate — intercepts before the Write
production_viz.mechanic: a two-lane diagram: left lane 'CLAUDE.md advisory' shows a probability dial and a Write call that sometimes passes through; right lane 'Hook enforcement' shows a gate/switch that physically blocks the Write call before execution, with a teal checkmark for blocked and crimson X on the other side

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two-lane comparison — advisory vs. enforcement gate

beats.narration_text: Maria runs the same session on a fresh project — one that has the hook configured. She asks Claude to summarize student performance. Claude proposes the Write. The hook fires. Exits non-zero: BLOCKED, grade pattern detected. The file is never written.

production_viz.label: the hook blocks the Write
production_viz.mechanic: Claude's proposed file content with 'Overall performance: B+' approaches the hook gate; the hook intercepts, a teal BLOCKED chip appears, and the file card shows empty — the Write never executes

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] hook intercepts and blocks the grade write

beats.narration_text: The hook is a script in .claude/hooks/, registered in settings.json. It reads the proposed tool input on stdin. Matches grade patterns. If it finds one, exits non-zero. Claude cannot override it. No prompt can persuade it. The rule is mechanical.
beats.scene_description: a printed terminal output pinned to newsprint showing the hook configuration — a settings.json snippet and the block-grades.sh script excerpt, editorial collage style, desaturated
beats.image_prompt: B09, printed terminal output showing a JSON configuration snippet and a bash script excerpt for a hook that blocks grade generation, pinned like a clipping to aged newsprint, desaturated editorial collage, flat print reproduction
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL ai] the hook configuration as a printed document

beats.narration_text: Here is where this bites. The grading tool is exactly the project where the stakes are real. Student work. FERPA. The school's grading process. A high-probability rule is not enough when the cost of the one violation is a parent email at 7pm asking why Claude assigned their child a C.

production_viz.label: stakes: one violation in 100 sessions
production_viz.mechanic: a grid of 100 session dots — 97 teal (rule followed), 3 crimson (violation); the 3 crimson dots are labeled 'grade leak', 'parent email', 'FERPA concern'; a serif label 'high probability is not enough here' beneath

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] stakes grid — high probability still means violations

beats.narration_text: The practical move: use the ask-Claude-to-write-the-hook pattern. Tell Claude what the hook must enforce, which tool event it covers, and what a clear error message looks like. Claude writes the script and the settings.json registration. You review the script, test by deliberately asking for a grade, and commit if it blocks.

production_viz.label: three steps: ask, review, test
production_viz.mechanic: three sequential cards appear left to right: 'Ask Claude to write the hook', 'Review the script', 'Test by triggering the block'; each card has a teal checkmark when completed

beats.narration_text: Maria's grading tool now has three hooks. Grade generation blocked. Verification runs after every edit. Session end writes an audit log. The rules that must hold every time — do. Every other rule stays in CLAUDE.md where it belongs.

production_viz.label: CLAUDE.md advisory vs. Hook deterministic — the boundary
production_viz.mechanic: two columns side by side; left 'CLAUDE.md' lists style rules in ink text; right 'Hooks' lists three must-hold rules in teal with a teal gate icon; a vertical dividing line labeled 'the boundary'

beats.narration_text: The heuristic: if violating this rule would be a real problem, it is a Hook. If violating it would be annoying, it stays in CLAUDE.md. The grading tool's never-rules have real consequences. They become hooks. The style rules stay advisory. Both layers compose.
CLAUDE.md says do not. A Hook says cannot. The grading tool's grade-generation rule used to be a request. Now it is a gate. The session where it failed was the last one.

card.copy: CLAUDE.md says do not. A Hook says cannot.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The heuristic: if violating this rule would be a real problem, it is a Hook. If violating it would be annoying, it stays in CLAUDE.md. The grading tool's never-rules have real consequences. They become hooks. The style rules stay advisory. Both layers compose. CLAUDE.md says do not. A Hook says cannot. The grading tool's grade-generation rule used to be a request. Now it is a gate. The session where it failed was the last one.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why 'Never Generate a Grade' Fails Until You Write a Hook] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
